---
title: A. Prerequisites
---

Before you can run the submission pipeline your machine needs six tools:
**Docker + Compose v2**, **Git**, the **GitHub CLI (`gh`)**, **make**,
**OpenSSL**, and **Python 3.12+**. Each section below gives the install command
for your OS and a verify step to confirm it worked.

::: {.callout-important}
You also need to have been **granted access** to the private BigPicture repos and
images first — see the [overview](index.qmd) and [GitHub access](github-access.qmd).
:::

::: {.callout-note}
## Linux: Ubuntu/Debian assumed
All Linux commands on this page use `apt`. On other distributions substitute
your package manager — `dnf` (Fedora/RHEL), `pacman` (Arch), `apk` (Alpine),
or `zypper` (openSUSE) — and adjust package names as needed.
:::

## Docker + Compose v2

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

Install **Docker Desktop** (includes Compose v2) with the package manager:

```powershell
winget install --id Docker.DockerDesktop -e
```

Then launch **Docker Desktop** once so the engine starts.

## macOS

Install **Docker Desktop** (includes Compose v2) with Homebrew:

```bash
brew install --cask docker
```

Then launch **Docker Desktop** once so the engine starts.

## Linux

Install **Docker Engine** and the **Compose v2 plugin** using these commands:

```bash
apt update
apt install -y curl
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com | sh
apt install -y docker-compose-plugin
```

Add your user to the `docker` group so you can run Docker without `sudo`:

```bash
usermod -aG docker $USER
```

Log out and back in for the group change to take effect.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify
```bash
docker --version
docker compose version
```

:::

## Git

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

```powershell
winget install --id Git.Git -e
```

Restart any open terminals after installing.

## macOS

```bash
brew install git
```

## Linux

```bash
apt install -y git
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
git --version
```

:::

## GitHub CLI (`gh`)

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

```powershell
winget install --id GitHub.cli -e
```

## macOS

```bash
brew install gh
```

## Linux

```bash
apt install gh
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
gh --version
```
:::

## make

On **Windows**, `make` is bundled with Git for Windows — no separate install.
You only need to add Git's tool folder to your system PATH once.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

Git for Windows ships `make` in `C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin`. Add that folder
to your system PATH:

1. Press the **Windows key**, type **environment variables**, and open
   **"Edit the system environment variables"**.
2. Click **Environment Variables…**
3. Under **System variables**, select **Path** and click **Edit…**
4. Click **New** and paste:
   ```
   C:\Program Files\Git\usr\bin
   ```
5. Click **OK** three times, then restart any open terminals.

::: {.callout-note}
If you installed Git to a custom location, replace `C:\Program Files\Git` with
your actual install path. The `openssl` command in the next section lives in the
same folder, so this one PATH change covers both.
:::

## macOS

Install GNU make via Homebrew and make it the default `make`:

```bash
brew install make
echo 'export PATH="$(brew --prefix)/opt/make/libexec/gnubin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

If you use Bash instead of Zsh, replace `~/.zshrc` with `~/.bash_profile`.

## Linux

```bash
apt install -y make
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
make --version
```

:::

## OpenSSL

On **Windows**, OpenSSL ships in the same Git folder you already added to PATH
in the make step — no extra install needed.

On **macOS**, the system `openssl` command is actually LibreSSL. The pipeline
needs real OpenSSL, so we install it via Homebrew.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

No additional install needed. Open a new terminal and confirm it is available:

```powershell
openssl version
```

## macOS

```bash
brew install openssl
echo 'export PATH="$(brew --prefix)/opt/openssl@3/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc
```

If you use Bash instead of Zsh, replace `~/.zshrc` with `~/.bash_profile`.

## Linux

```bash
apt install -y openssl
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
openssl version
```

:::

## Python

The pipeline requires **Python 3.12 or later**.

::: {.panel-tabset}
## Windows

```powershell
winget install --id Python.Python.3.12 -e
```

Restart any open terminals after installing.

## macOS

```bash
brew install python@3.12
```

## Linux

On **Ubuntu 24.04 and later**, Python 3.12 is in the default repositories:

```bash
apt install -y python3.12
```

On **Ubuntu 22.04**, install it via the deadsnakes PPA:

```bash
add-apt-repository ppa:deadsnakes/ppa
apt update
apt install -y python3.12
```
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
## Verify

```bash
python3.12 --version
```

On Windows, use `python --version` instead.

:::

Once every tool reports a version, continue to [B. GitHub access](github-access.qmd).
